observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. City Accountant, in coordination with the City Treasurer, to review the list
of stale checks to determine that the checks had not been replaced,
particularly those significant in amount such as those payable to the GSIS, Meralco and LBP amounting to P4,693,289.22, P3,183,107.14, P2,267,520.00, respectively, and for
claims which are determined to be still valid to date, to prepare accounting entries to
take up the cancellation of stale checks and restoration of the accounts payable,
subject to compliance with the guidelines prescribed under Section 59 of the MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume I.
b. City Treasurer to henceforth prepare and send written notice to the payees of the
existence of their checks at least one month before a check becomes stale.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant and the City Treasurer promised to comply with the audit
recommendations.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the concerned personnel to write the
City’s depository banks to remind them of their obligation under PD No. 1445 to furnish
the City with pertinent bank statements at the close of each month to enable the City
Accountant to prepare the BRSs and submit copies to the Audit Team for verification
within ten days after the end of each month.

Management’s Comment:
The City Mayor will write the City’s depository banks to remind them of their
obligation to furnish the City with the bank statements at the close of each month to
enable the City Accountant to prepare the BRSs for submission of copies to the
Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to draw
Journal Entry Voucher to take up in the books the reconciling items identified
during the process of bank reconciliation with net amount of P1,059,823.87.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant will take up in the books the reconciling items with net amount
of P1,059,823.87.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a) Inventory Committee to –
• prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for
approval of the City Mayor to ensure that the conduct of inventory is
properly and adequately planned;
• submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA at least 10 calendar days before
the scheduled start of inventory taking activities; and
• complete the conduct of physical count of all PPE at least once a year and
submit the RPCPPE classified per category/type to the Office of the Auditor not
later than January 31 of each year.

b) City General Services Officer (CGSO) to update the PCs based on the result of the
physical inventory and maintain them per category of PPE with complete
information about the PPE; and

c) CGSO and City Accounting Office to reconcile the balances of their respective
records of PPE.

Management’s Comment:
The Inventory Committee will submit the PIP to the Audit Team in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2020-006, complete the physical inventory taking of PPE and submit the
RPCPPE. Likewise, the CGSO will update the PCs and reconcile her records with the
City Accounting Office.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare
correcting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts
by debiting Advances to Contractors in the amount of P62,100,000.00 and
crediting Construction in Progress - Buildings and Other Structures of the same
amount.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant had prepared the correcting journal entries on April 4, 2022.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. instruct the City Accountant to observe the proper accounting treatment,
classification and recording of procurement of medical, dental and laboratory
supplies, computers and printers, furniture and fixtures, and medical
equipment in accordance with The Revised Chart of Accounts provided under
COA Circular No. 2015-009 and COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. require the City Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to reflect the
correct balances of the affected accounts by debiting Medical Equipment,
Information and Communication Technology Equipment, Furniture and
Fixtures, and Office Equipment accounts in the amounts of P4,544,800.00,
P140,250.00, P201,000.00 and P4,180,137.00, respectively, and crediting
Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory account and Other
Supplies and Materials Expenses account in the amounts P1,630,050.00 and
P7,436,137.00, respectively.
c. instruct the City General Services Officer to also reflect the adjustments in the
Medical Equipment, Information and Communication Technology Equipment,
Furniture and Fixtures, and Office Equipment in the Report of Physical Count
of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and their respective Property
Cards.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant had prepared the adjusting journal entries on April 4, 2022.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant to prepare
adjusting journal entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts by
debiting Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment and crediting Construction
and Heavy Equipment accounts both for P26,540,000.00.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant had prepared the adjusting journal entries on April 4, 2022.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and the Bids
and Awards Committee Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team, for further
evaluation, the lacking documents pertaining to the subject expenditures with
total amount of P90,798,162.10 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit
and thereafter ensure that all documentary requirements are attached to the
disbursement vouchers prior to payment to establish the validity and propriety of
the claim.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant will submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development
Coordinator and other concerned officials to prepare a catch-up plan on projects
that had been stalled or delayed due to pandemic-linked lockdowns in the
country.

Management’s Comment:
The City Planning and Development Officer explained that projects in the total amount
of P115,500,000.00 were re-programmed to COVID-19 related program, projects and
activities which will be fully implemented in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Engineer to complete and
update the information on the installed signboards for the on-going infrastructure
projects by indicating the start date of the construction and target date of
completion as required by COA Circular No. 2013-004.

Management’s Comment:
The City Engineer agreed with the audit observations and assured that project
information will be reflected on the signboards as required by COA Circular No. 2013-
004.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the respective Chairpersons of the
BAC for goods and infrastructure projects to invite the Auditors and other
observers in writing to also observe the bid evaluation and post-qualification
proceedings of the BAC pursuant to Section 13.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA
No. 9184.

Management’s Comment:
The BAC Chairpersons for goods and infrastructure projects explained that formal
invitation in writing to observers were given for the opening bids, however, only verbal
invitations were given for the conduct of bid evaluation and post qualification. They will
correct the practice as recommended by the Auditors and as such, the BAC
Chairpersons will provide consolidated written invitations to the Auditors and other
observers that will also include bid evaluation and post-qualification.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit
immediately to the COA Audit Team the 11 DVs, including the supporting
documents, totaling P2,147,406.85 to facilitate the audit thereof, otherwise the
disbursements will be suspended in audit.

Henceforth, it is recommended that the City Accountant be directed to submit to
the Audit Team within 10 days from receipt all paid vouchers and their supporting
documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.

Management’s Comment:
The City Accountant promised to locate the remaining 11 DVs for immediate
submission to the COA Audit Team.

We recommended that The City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare BRSs for all depository accounts and submit to the Audit Team for verification within ten days after the end of each month to ascertain the reliability of the Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant had submitted the BRSs for 16 depository accounts of the City for the month of December 2020 but she was not able to submit the BRSs for CY 2021 of this report.
observations.reason:
Concerned officials did not provide explanation on non-implementation of the recommendation.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page No. 38 Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and accordingly conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE of the City and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE following the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. City General Services Officer (CGSO) to update her property records based on the RPCPPE; and
c. CGSO and City Accountant to closely work together to reconcile their respective property records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the City’s PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee had prepared the comprehensive Plan but the physical inventory was still incomplete. The CGSO had updated her property records based on the RPCPPE for motor vehicles only. The CGSO and City Accountant had reconciled their respective property records for motor vehicles only.
observations.reason:
Concerned officials did not provide explanation on non-implementation of the audit recommendation.
The RPCPPE was still incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year's recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to review the RPT and SET Receivables accounts, including retracing of the previous years’ entries and reports, and accordingly make the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, ensure that the balances of the two receivables are equal and correct at the end of the year. We further recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the error in recording RPT collections in CY 2020 by debiting RPT Receivables and crediting Deferred RPT both in the amount of P19,259,595.33.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant had drawn JEV to correct the error in recording RPT collections in CY 2020 by debiting RPT Receivables and crediting Deferred RPT both in the amount of P19,259,595.33.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to draw JEV to correct the error in recording the transfer of unexpended balance of the DRRMF – QRF from General Fund to Special Trust Fund by debiting Other Payables and crediting Trust Liability – DRRMF both in the amount of P27,692,609.25. We further recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to provide sufficient disclosure of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant had drawn the JEV to adjust the error in recording transfer of DRRMF.
observations.reason: The City provided sufficient disclosure of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to disclose the required information in the NTFS to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with the additional information value.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant provided the required disclosure in the NTFS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the Orders or any equivalent document showing authority of the personnel/job order workers to report for work during ECQ within 15 days after receipt of the report. Also submit the approved DTRs of claimants during said quarantine period to determine correctness of the amount paid for COVID-19 Hazard Pay; and b. City Budget Officer and City Accountant to properly account the COVID-19 hazard pay of regular employees under Personnel Services to reflect the correct expenses incurred in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted the Orders or any required documents to the Audit Team. The COVID-19 Hazard Pay was now properly accounted for under Personnel Services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. City Accountant to properly record the purchases and distribution of Welfare Goods for Distribution and to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for all their receipts, issuances and balances; and b. CDRRMO/CGSO to maintain Stock Cards to record the purchases and issuances of welfare goods. The issuances of these items should be supported with the RIS and reported monthly to the Accounting Office thru the preparation and submission of monthly RSMI as basis of the City Accountant in recording issued items under account Welfare Goods Expenses.
observations.managementAction: The CGSO is already maintaining stock cards and issuing RIS to record purchases and issuance of welfare goods. The issuances of these items are supported with the RIS and reported monthly to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to submit to the Audit Team the LDRRMF’s approved LDRRMFIP within 15 days from receipt of the report to avoid suspension of the transactions. Henceforth, it is recommended that the City Accountant ensure that the disbursements of LDRRMF out of the STF are supported with LDRRMC’s approved LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant provided the LDRRMC’s approved LDRRMFIP covering all COVID-19 related PPAs in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the: a. BAC Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team justifications on its inability to comply with the posting requirement under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020, for further evaluation; and b. BAC Secretariat and the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the suppliers’ Income/Business Tax Return of the suppliers’ pertaining to COVID-19 expenditures totaling P283,120,418.90, to avoid issuance of audit suspension.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat and the City Accountant submitted the suppliers’ Income/Business Tax Return of the suppliers’ pertaining to COVID-19 expenditures totaling P283,120,418.90, to avoid issuance of audit suspension.
observations.reason: Posting requirement was not complied due to the implementation of hard lockdown.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC and BAC Secretariat to: a. submit relevant documents and explanation to the Audit Team why the suppliers’ private contracts on the procurement of rice repacking machine and various decorations were awarded to the suppliers despite non-submission of their contracts that were similar to the contracts to be bid and with values equivalent to 50 per cent and 25 per cent of ABC, respectively, within 15 days from receipt of the report for further evaluation, otherwise the amount of P11,346,800.00 will be disallowed in audit; and b. henceforth evaluate the bid documents submitted by the bidders meticulously and recommend the award of the contracts only to eligible and responsive bidders.
observations.managementAction: The concerned City officials had submitted the suppliers’ private contracts on the procurement of the rice repacking machine and various decorations to support why the contract with amount exceeding 50 per cent of the subject ABC was awarded to said suppliers. Contracts were awarded only to eligible and responsive bidders.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to deduct the warranty security in the form of retention money from claims of the suppliers or requiring the suppliers to post special bank guaranty equivalent to one per cent of the contract price being required under Section 62.1 of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant is now deducting the warranty security in the form of retention money from claims of the suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant and other concerned officials to enforce the collection of liquidated damages of P327,321.08 from the concerned suppliers and to take the necessary legal remedies should the suppliers refuse to voluntarily settle the amount. Henceforth, we recommended that the City Accountant strictly comply with Item No. 3 of Annex D of the RIRR by computing and deducting the amount of liquidated damages from claims of suppliers who incur delays in the delivery of goods to the City.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant is complying with the requirement of Item No. 3 of Annex D of the RIRR by computing and deducting the amount of liquidated damages from claims of suppliers who incur delays in the delivery of goods to the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to refrain from granting further additional cash advances to personnel with outstanding cash advances and to institute measures to ensure that CAs are liquidated on time.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation of cash advances were made promptly and granting of additional CAs to personnel with outstanding CAs were discontinued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit immediately to the Audit Team the unsubmitted 1,886 DVs including their supporting documents totaling P695,715,875.48 within 15 days after receipt of the report for further evaluation, otherwise the disbursements will be suspended in audit. Henceforth, it is recommended that the City Accountant be directed to submit to the Audit Team within 10 days from receipt all paid vouchers and their supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant had timely submitted DVs and supporting documents except for unclaimed checks.
observations.reason: Inability of the concerned personnel to record transactions promptly, thus incurring delay in the submission of DVs and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the LRs for CAs including the supporting documents totaling P687,571,836.60 within 15 days after receipt of the report for further evaluation, otherwise the transactions will be suspended in audit. Henceforth, it is recommended that the City Accountant be directed to submit to the Audit Team within 10 days from receipt of all Liquidation Reports and their supporting documents as required by COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant is now submitting the LRs for CAs including supporting documents to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to strictly comply with BIR RMC No. 23-2012 by ensuring that the amounts of all taxes withheld are remitted in full on or before the 15th day of the following month to prevent incurrence of surcharges and penalties. We likewise recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to cause the immediate remittance of the amount of P20,400,862.86 to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant caused the immediate remittance of the amount of P20,400,862.86 to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to: a. strictly comply with the IRR of RA No. 8291, RA No. 9679 and HDMF Circular No. 275 by ensuring that the amounts withheld from employees are remitted in full within the prescribed period to avoid payment of penalties for the late remittance; and b. cause the immediate remittance of the amount of P4,900,434.22 and P249,605.08 to the GSIS and HDMF, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant caused the full remittance of P4,900,434.22 and P249,605.08 to the GSIS and HDMF, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Planning and Development Officer and other concerned City officials to plan, discuss and identify the COVID-19 related PPAs which need urgent implementation and prepare and submit the necessary reports for consideration of the Local Development Council in the supplemental investment program to be approved by the Sanggunian Panlungsod.
observations.managementAction: The funds were either utilized or reprogrammed to other COVID-19 related programs and projects. The City Planning and Development Officer and other concerned City officials planned, discussed and identified the COVID-19 related PPAs which need urgent implementation and prepared the necessary reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to: a. determine gender issues on the attributed projects of the City based on thorough gender analysis as required under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended; and b. incorporate the implementation of attributed projects in the GAD AR to ascertain the effectiveness of GAD Fund in promoting gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s rights, and promoting women’s empowerment.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person had undertaken gender analysis as required under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. The GAD Focal Person had incorporated the implementation of attributed projects in the GAD AR.
